pattern: Counting each bit position modulo three isolates the lone element, since every tripled number contributes a multiple of three to each bit.
hints: Every value except one appears three times; think about what that does to each individual bit across the whole array.
For a fixed bit position, the count of set bits among all numbers is a multiple of three plus whatever the unique number contributes there.
Sum the set bits at each of the 32 positions, take each sum modulo three, and rebuild the number; remember to sign-extend bits at and above position 31.

edge_inputs.description: larger unique value at the end
unique element is negative among positives
extreme 32-bit values, unique is min int
unique zero surrounded by tripled values

stress.description: large array built by tripling random values plus one unique - exercises the bit counting
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    triples = (n - 1) // 3
    vals = []
    used = set()
    for _ in range(triples):
        v = rng.randrange(-2**31, 2**31)
        vals.extend([v, v, v])
        used.add(v)
    u = rng.randrange(-2**31, 2**31)
    while u in used:
        u = rng.randrange(-2**31, 2**31)
    vals.append(u)
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    return [vals]
stress.note: every value appears three times except the planted unique
